# ASRS - Agent Software Requirements Specification

> A specification language for describing software systems, designed for humans and AI agents.

## Quick Start

- [ASRS Meta-Spec](spec/meta.yaml) - ASRS describing itself (YAML format)
- [Examples](examples/) - Example ASRS documents in YAML
- [Validator](tools/asrs-validator.py) - Validate ASRS documents
- [Converter](tools/asrs-convert.py) - Convert between ASRS and YAML formats

## Specification

- [1. Purpose](spec/01-purpose.md) - What ASRS is and who it's for
- [2. Normative Language](spec/02-normative-language.md) - RFC 2119 keywords
- [3. Design Principles](spec/03-design-principles.md) - Agent Native, Minimal Vocabulary, Behavior First
- [4. Versioning](spec/04-versioning.md) - Semantic Versioning 2.0
- [5. Standards References](spec/05-standards.md) - RFC 2119, SemVer, ISO 29148, Gherkin
- [6. Core Entities](spec/06-core-entities.md) - Project, Principal, Feature, Scenario, Component, Resource, Test
- [7. Grammar](spec/07-grammar.md) - Abstract grammar and format mapping
- [8. Canonical Style](spec/08-canonical-style.md) - Formatting rules
- [9. Identity](spec/09-identity.md) - ID format and uniqueness
- [10. Entity Specification](spec/10-entity-specification.md) - Detailed entity definitions
- [11. References](spec/11-references.md) - Relationship properties
- [12. Verification](spec/12-verification.md) - Correctness definitions
- [13. Traceability](spec/13-traceability.md) - Entity relationship chain
- [14. Validation Rules](spec/14-validation-rules.md) - Document validity requirements
- [15. Compliance Levels](spec/15-compliance-levels.md) - Level 1, 2, 3
- [16. Complete Example](spec/16-complete-example.md) - Full example document
- [17. Format Specification](spec/17-format-specification.md) - YAML, JSON, TOML formats

## Core Entities

| Entity | Purpose | Required Properties |
|--------|---------|---------------------|
| Project | Root of specification | Name, Specification, Document Version |
| Principal | External entity interacting with system | Type |
| Feature | System capability | (none) |
| Scenario | Observable behavior | Given, When, Then |
| Component | Implementation unit | Type |
| Resource | Persistent data | Type |
| Test | Executable verification | Validates, Given, When, Then |

## Formats

| Format | Extension | Status |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| YAML | `.yaml` | Primary |
| JSON | `.json` | Planned |
| TOML | `.toml` | Planned |

## Tools

- [ASRS Validator](tools/asrs-validator.py) - Validate ASRS documents
- [ASRS Converter](tools/asrs-convert.py) - Convert between YAML, JSON, TOML formats

## Examples (YAML Format)

- [Login System](examples/login.yaml) - Simple authentication
- [Book Library](examples/books.yaml) - Multi-user role system
- [URL Shortener](examples/url-shortener.yaml) - API-focused service

## Versioning

- [Modification Log](examples/.asrs.log) - Track all changes

## Documentation

- [SRS Comparison](docs/srs-comparison.md) - How ASRS compares to traditional SRS
- [Agent Guidelines](docs/agent-guidelines.md) - Guidelines for AI agents working with ASRS

## External References

- [RFC 2119](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2119.txt) - Key words for requirements
- [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/) - Version numbering
- [ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148](https://www.iso.org/standard/72368.html) - Requirements engineering
- [Gherkin](https://cucumber.io/docs/gherkin/) - Behavior-driven syntax
- [Agent Skills](https://agentskills.io/) - Skill format specification
